title: Bridge Essay: Domain-specific and domain-general processes underlying metacognitive judgments.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Metacognition — self-awareness, self-monitoring, neural correlates of meta-awareness.

**Domain-specific and domain-general processes underlying metacognitive judgments.**. Conscious Cogn.. PMID: 28222381. DOI: 10.1016/j.concog.2017.01.011. Metacognition and self-awareness are commonly assumed to operate as global capacities. However, there have been few attempts to test this assumption across multiple cognitive domains and metacognitive evaluations. Here, we assessed the covariance between "online" metacognitive processes, as measured by decision confidence judgments in the domains of perception and memory, and error awareness in the domain of attention to action.
